text:
'She was never happy at home, Miss Alice wasn't, from the time that her father married again. She was slighted like and had no say in anything, but it never really became bad for her until after she met Mr. Fowler at a friend's house. As well as I could learn, Miss Alice had rights of her own by will, but she was so quiet and patient, she was, that she never said a word about them but just left everything in Mr. Rucastle's hands. He knew he was safe with her; but when there was a chance of a husband coming forward, who would ask for all that the law would give him, then her father thought it time to put a stop on it. He wanted her to sign a paper, so that whether she married or not, he could use her money. When she wouldn't do it, he kept on worrying her until she got brain-fever, and for six weeks was at death's door. Then she got better at last, all worn to a shadow, and with her beautiful hair cut off; but that didn't make no change in her young man, and he stuck to her as true as man could be.'  
'Ah,' said Holmes, 'I think that what you have been good enough to tell us makes the matter fairly clear, and that I can deduce all that remains. Mr. Rucastle then, I presume, took to this system of imprisonment?'  
'Yes, sir.'  
'And brought Miss Hunter down from London in order to get rid of the disagreeable persistence of Mr. Fowler.'  
'That was it, sir.'  
'But Mr. Fowler being a persevering man, as a good seaman should be, blockaded the house, and having met you succeeded by certain arguments, metallic or otherwise, in convincing you that your interests were the same as his.'  
'Mr. Fowler was a very kind-spoken, free-handed gentleman,' said Mrs. Toller serenely.  
'And in this way he managed that your good man should have no want of drink, and that a ladder should be ready at the moment when your master had gone out.'  
'You have it, sir, just as it happened.'  
'I am sure we owe you an apology, Mrs. Toller,' said Holmes, 'for you have certainly cleared up everything which puzzled us. And here comes the country surgeon and Mrs. Rucastle, so I think, Watson, that we had best escort Miss Hunter back to Winchester, as it seems to me that our locus standi now is rather a questionable one.'  
And thus was solved the mystery of the sinister house with the copper beeches in front of the door. Mr. Rucastle survived, but was always a broken man, kept alive solely through the care of his devoted wife. They still live with their old servants, who probably know so much of Rucastle's past life that he finds it difficult to part from them. Mr. Fowler and Miss Rucastle were married, by special license, in Southampton the day after their flight, and he is now the holder of a government appointment in the island of Mauritius. As to Miss Violet Hunter, my friend Holmes, rather to my disappointment, manifested no further interest in her when once she had ceased to be the centre of one of his problems, and she is now the head of a private school at Walsall, where I believe that she has met with considerable success.
name: Chunk 8/8

raw_entities.name: Alice Rucastle
raw_entities.description: The central victim of the narrative, subjected to financial coercion and psychological abuse by her stepfather. She suffers a severe illness and physical alteration but remains steadfast in her devotion to her suitor, ultimately escaping to secure her legal rights and marry.
context: She was never happy at home, Miss Alice wasn't
Mr. Rucastle then, I presume, took to this system of imprisonment?
Mr. Fowler and Miss Rucastle were married, by special license, in Southampton the day after their flight

raw_entities.name: Mr. Rucastle
raw_entities.description: Alice's stepfather who seizes control of her inherited wealth and employs a system of imprisonment to prevent her marriage. He survives the events but is left a broken man entirely dependent on his wife's care.
context: left everything in Mr. Rucastle's hands
Mr. Rucastle then, I presume, took to this system of imprisonment?
Mr. Rucastle survived, but was always a broken man, kept alive solely through the care of his devoted wife.

raw_entities.name: Mr. Fowler
raw_entities.description: A persevering sailor and Alice's suitor who orchestrates her rescue by bribing household staff and arranging an escape route. He successfully marries Alice and secures a government post in Mauritius following their flight.
context: met Mr. Fowler at a friend's house
Mr. Fowler being a persevering man, as a good seaman should be, blockaded the house
Mr. Fowler was a very kind-spoken, free-handed gentleman

raw_entities.name: Miss Violet Hunter
raw_entities.description: A woman connected to the case who is brought from London to help resolve the mystery. After the case concludes, Holmes loses interest in her, and she establishes herself as a successful headmistress of a private school.
context: brought Miss Hunter down from London in order to get rid of the disagreeable persistence of Mr. Fowler.
As to Miss Violet Hunter, my friend Holmes, rather to my disappointment, manifested no further interest in her when once she had ceased to be the centre of one of his problems

raw_entities.name: Mrs. Toller
raw_entities.description: A household servant who collaborates with Mr. Fowler to facilitate Alice's escape. She is motivated by financial gain and alcohol, and her testimony provides the crucial details that clear up the mystery for Holmes.
context: said Mrs. Toller serenely.
I am sure we owe you an apology, Mrs. Toller,

raw_entities.name: Holmes
raw_entities.description: The detective who deduces the plot of imprisonment and financial coercion from Mrs. Toller's account. He resolves the mystery, apologizes to the household staff, and departs with Watson once their legal standing becomes questionable.
context: Ah," said Holmes, "I think that what you have been good enough to tell us makes the matter fairly clear
I am sure we owe you an apology, Mrs. Toller," said Holmes

raw_entities.name: Watson
raw_entities.description: The narrator and companion to Holmes, who assists in escorting Miss Hunter back to Winchester after the case is solved and the investigators' presence becomes legally questionable.
context: so I think, Watson, that we had best escort Miss Hunter back to Winchester

raw_entities.name: Mrs. Rucastle
raw_entities.description: Mr. Rucastle's wife, who is present when Holmes and Watson conclude their investigation. She remains devoted to her husband, providing the care that keeps him alive after he is left a broken man.
context: And here comes the country surgeon and Mrs. Rucastle
kept alive solely through the care of his devoted wife.

raw_entities.name: The Sinister House
raw_entities.description: The primary setting where Alice is imprisoned and where the conspiracy to control her wealth unfolds. Characterized by copper beeches at the entrance, it serves as the physical locus of the mystery and the site of the blockade and escape.
context: She was never happy at home
blockaded the house
mystery of the sinister house with the copper beeches in front of the door.

context: brought Miss Hunter down from London in order to get rid of the disagreeable persistence of Mr. Fowler.

raw_entities.name: Winchester
raw_entities.description: A city where Holmes and Watson plan to escort Miss Hunter after resolving the case, indicating the geographical setting of the investigation and the destination for the characters' departure.
context: escort Miss Hunter back to Winchester

raw_entities.name: Southampton
raw_entities.description: The city where Mr. Fowler and Miss Rucastle are married by special license immediately after their escape, marking the formalization of their union and the resolution of the central conflict.
context: Mr. Fowler and Miss Rucastle were married, by special license, in Southampton the day after their flight

raw_entities.name: Island of Mauritius
raw_entities.description: A distant island where Mr. Fowler holds a government appointment following his marriage to Alice, representing his successful resolution of the conflict and the establishment of a new life abroad.
context: he is now the holder of a government appointment in the island of Mauritius.

raw_entities.name: Walsall
raw_entities.description: A town where Miss Violet Hunter eventually becomes the head of a private school, marking her independent career and post-case life after the mystery is resolved.
context: she is now the head of a private school at Walsall

raw_entities.description: A legal document Mr. Rucastle forces Alice to sign to secure control over her inherited wealth. It serves as the central instrument of financial coercion and the primary motive for the imprisonment.
context: He wanted her to sign a paper, so that whether she married or not, he could use her money.

raw_entities.name: The Imprisonment System
raw_entities.description: The method employed by Mr. Rucastle to confine Alice and prevent her from claiming her legal rights or marrying. It forms the core conflict of the narrative and is the primary subject of Holmes's deduction.
context: Mr. Rucastle then, I presume, took to this system of imprisonment?
And thus was solved the mystery of the sinister house

raw_entities.name: The Blockade
raw_entities.description: Mr. Fowler's strategic action of surrounding the house to pressure the household staff and facilitate Alice's escape. It represents the active intervention that breaks the stepfather's control.
context: blockaded the house, and having met you succeeded by certain arguments

raw_entities.name: Brain-fever
raw_entities.description: A severe illness Alice contracts after prolonged psychological and physical abuse by her stepfather. It leaves her physically weakened, bald, and at death's door, marking a turning point in her suffering.
context: he kept on worrying her until she got brain-fever, and for six weeks was at death's door.

context: he is now the holder of a government appointment in the island of Mauritius.

raw_entities.description: An educational institution in Walsall where Miss Violet Hunter becomes headmistress, marking her independent career and post-case life after the mystery is resolved.

raw_entities.name: Locus Standi
raw_entities.description: A legal term referring to the right to bring an action or take part in a lawsuit, which Holmes questions regarding their continued presence at the house after the mystery is solved, prompting their departure.
context: as it seems to me that our locus standi now is rather a questionable one.

context: Miss Alice had rights of her own by will, but she was so quiet and patient
